name: Schema migration runner + 013 collision fix
description: Create migration 019_schema_migrations.sql with schema_migrations table (version INT PK, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ, filename TEXT). Overhaul scripts/setup_db.sh to: (1) create DB if not exists, (2) ensure pgvector extension, (3) create schema_migrations if not exists, (4) introspect existing tables to backfill schema_migrations for already-applied migrations, (5) apply each unapplied migration in order checking schema_migrations first. Fix 013 collision by renaming 013_query_log.sql to 016_query_log.sql (016 already exists as the query_log migration). The runner must be idempotent — running against a DB with all 18 existing migrations already applied produces 18 rows in schema_migrations and zero errors.
acceptance: File migrations/019_schema_migrations.sql exists and creates schema_migrations table with columns: version INT PK, applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), filename TEXT NOT NULL
scripts/setup_db.sh iterates all migrations/*.sql in numeric order, checks schema_migrations before applying each one, and records applied migrations
The 013 numbering collision is resolved: only one file has prefix 013
Running setup_db.sh against a database with all existing migrations already applied results in rows in schema_migrations for each migration and zero errors (backfill works)
Running setup_db.sh twice in succession produces no errors and no duplicate rows
pytest tests/test_migration_runner.py passes with 0 failures

name: Shared HTTP client with retry, rate-limit, circuit breaker
description: Create src/scix/http_client.py providing a ResilientClient class with: (1) configurable retry count with exponential backoff (default 3 retries, base 1s), (2) per-host rate limiting via token bucket (configurable requests/second per host), (3) circuit breaker pattern (fail-open after N consecutive failures, configurable threshold default 5, reset after configurable cooldown default 60s), (4) response caching to disk (configurable cache dir, keyed by URL+params hash, TTL-based expiry), (5) configurable User-Agent header (default 'scix-harvester/1.0'). Must be usable as a drop-in replacement for requests.get() with a .get() method. All configuration via constructor parameters with sensible defaults. Tests must cover retry with mock 503, rate limiting enforcement, circuit breaker open/close transitions, cache hit/miss, and User-Agent header.
acceptance: File src/scix/http_client.py exists with ResilientClient class
ResilientClient.get(url) retries on 5xx with exponential backoff; test with mock 503 shows 3 retries with increasing delays
Per-host rate limiting enforced: 2 rapid requests to same host with rate_limit=1/s shows second request delayed
Circuit breaker opens after N consecutive failures and raises CircuitBreakerOpen; closes after cooldown
Response caching to disk works: second identical request returns cached response without HTTP call
User-Agent header set on all requests (default 'scix-harvester/1.0')
pytest tests/test_http_client.py passes with 0 failures

name: Harvest runs tracking table
description: Create migration 020_harvest_runs.sql with harvest_runs table: id SERIAL PK, source TEXT NOT NULL, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), finished_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'running', records_fetched INT DEFAULT 0, records_upserted INT DEFAULT 0, cursor_state JSONB, error_message TEXT, config JSONB, counts JSONB DEFAULT '{}'. Add index on source. Write tests verifying the table exists, can insert/update rows, and status transitions work. This table is referenced by entity-graph-schema via FK.
acceptance: File migrations/020_harvest_runs.sql exists with valid SQL creating harvest_runs table
Table has columns: id SERIAL PK, source TEXT NOT NULL, started_at, finished_at, status, records_fetched, records_upserted, cursor_state JSONB, error_message TEXT, config JSONB, counts JSONB
Index exists on harvest_runs(source)
INSERT + UPDATE lifecycle works: insert with status='running', update to status='completed' with counts
pytest tests/test_harvest_runs.py passes with 0 failures

name: Entity graph tables + compat view + seed migration
description: Create migration 021_entity_graph.sql with: (1) entities table: id SERIAL PK, canonical_name TEXT NOT NULL, entity_type TEXT NOT NULL, discipline TEXT, source TEXT NOT NULL, harvest_run_id INT REFERENCES harvest_runs(id), properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(). UNIQUE(canonical_name, entity_type, source). Indexes on entity_type, discipline, lower(canonical_name). GIN index on properties with jsonb_path_ops. (2) entity_identifiers: entity_id INT REFERENCES entities(id), id_scheme TEXT NOT NULL, external_id TEXT NOT NULL, is_primary BOOLEAN DEFAULT false. PK(id_scheme, external_id). Index on entity_id. (3) entity_aliases: entity_id INT REFERENCES entities(id), alias TEXT NOT NULL, alias_source TEXT. PK(entity_id, alias). Functional index on lower(alias). (4) entity_relationships: id SERIAL PK, subject_entity_id INT REFERENCES entities(id), predicate TEXT NOT NULL, object_entity_id INT REFERENCES entities(id), source TEXT, harvest_run_id INT REFERENCES harvest_runs(id), confidence REAL DEFAULT 1.0. UNIQUE(subject_entity_id, predicate, object_entity_id). Index on object_entity_id. (5) document_entities: bibcode TEXT NOT NULL, entity_id INT REFERENCES entities(id), link_type TEXT NOT NULL, confidence REAL, match_method TEXT, evidence JSONB, harvest_run_id INT REFERENCES harvest_runs(id). PK(bibcode, entity_id, link_type). NO FK on bibcode. (6) datasets: id SERIAL PK, name TEXT NOT NULL, discipline TEXT, source TEXT NOT NULL, canonical_id TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, temporal_start DATE, temporal_end DATE, properties JSONB DEFAULT '{}', harvest_run_id INT REFERENCES harvest_runs(id), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(). UNIQUE(source, canonical_id). (7) dataset_entities: dataset_id INT REFERENCES datasets(id), entity_id INT REFERENCES entities(id), relationship TEXT NOT NULL. PK(dataset_id, entity_id, relationship). (8) document_datasets: bibcode TEXT NOT NULL, dataset_id INT REFERENCES datasets(id), link_type TEXT NOT NULL, confidence REAL, match_method TEXT, harvest_run_id INT REFERENCES harvest_runs(id). PK(bibcode, dataset_id, link_type). (9) entity_dictionary_compat view exposing same columns as entity_dictionary (id, canonical_name, entity_type, source, external_id, aliases, metadata) from entities + entity_aliases + entity_identifiers. (10) Seed migration: INSERT INTO entities SELECT ... FROM entity_dictionary to give ~55K entities on day one, plus copy aliases and external_ids into entity_aliases and entity_identifiers.
acceptance: File migrations/021_entity_graph.sql exists with valid SQL
\d entities shows all columns, constraints, and indexes as specified in PRD
\d entity_identifiers shows PK(id_scheme, external_id) and entity_id index
\d entity_aliases shows PK(entity_id, alias) and functional index on lower(alias)
\d entity_relationships shows UNIQUE(subject_entity_id, predicate, object_entity_id) and index on object_entity_id
\d document_entities shows PK(bibcode, entity_id, link_type) with NO FK on bibcode
\d datasets shows UNIQUE(source, canonical_id)
entity_dictionary_compat view exists and returns rows with same column names as entity_dictionary
Seed migration: SELECT count(*) FROM entities returns approximately count(*) from entity_dictionary (within 5%)
Entity aliases from entity_dictionary.aliases[] are copied to entity_aliases table
Entity external_ids from entity_dictionary.external_id are copied to entity_identifiers table
pytest tests/test_entity_graph_schema.py passes with 0 failures

name: Staging tables for entity graph
description: Create migration 022_staging_entities.sql that adds staging counterparts for entity graph tables: staging.entities (mirror of public.entities without FK to harvest_runs), staging.entity_identifiers (mirror without FK), staging.entity_aliases (mirror without FK). Create staging.promote_entities() function that atomically promotes entities + identifiers + aliases in a single transaction (not single-table like staging.promote_extractions). The promote function must: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT for entities, then INSERT ... ON CONFLICT for identifiers and aliases using the newly-inserted entity IDs, then TRUNCATE staging tables. Add counts JSONB validation: after promote, verify alias/identifier counts are proportional to entity counts.
acceptance: File migrations/022_staging_entities.sql exists with valid SQL
staging.entities table exists mirroring public.entities structure
staging.entity_identifiers and staging.entity_aliases tables exist
staging.promote_entities() function exists and promotes all 3 tables atomically in one transaction
After promote: public.entities has rows, staging tables are empty
Promote handles ON CONFLICT correctly (upsert, not error on duplicates)
pytest tests/test_staging_entities.py passes with 0 failures

name: GCMD harvester update → entities table + http_client
description: Update scripts/harvest_gcmd.py to: (1) use ResilientClient from src/scix/http_client.py for all HTTP requests instead of raw requests, (2) write to new entities table (in addition to entity_dictionary for backward compat), (3) store GCMD UUID in entity_identifiers with id_scheme='gcmd_uuid', (4) log harvest_run to harvest_runs table with accurate counts. The harvester should populate entities.discipline='earth_science', entities.source='gcmd', and store GCMD hierarchy path in entities.properties. Must maintain backward compatibility: entity_dictionary rows still created. Update tests to verify both old and new table writes.
acceptance: scripts/harvest_gcmd.py imports and uses ResilientClient from src/scix/http_client
After harvest, SELECT count(*) FROM entities WHERE source='gcmd' returns >0 matching entity_dictionary counts
All GCMD entities have entity_identifiers rows with id_scheme='gcmd_uuid'
harvest_runs has row with source='gcmd' and status='completed' and accurate records_upserted count
entity_dictionary rows still created (backward compatibility maintained)
All entries have discipline='earth_science' in entities table
pytest tests/test_harvest_gcmd.py passes with 0 failures

name: PDS4 harvester update → entities + relationships

Must maintain backward compatibility with entity_dictionary. OSIRIS-REx mission should have relationships to its instruments (OCAMS, OLA, OTES, OVIRS, REXIS) and Bennu target.

After harvest, entities table has missions, instruments, targets with discipline='planetary_science'
PDS URN stored in entity_identifiers with id_scheme='pds_urn'
entity_relationships has rows with predicates: 'part_of_mission', 'observes_target'
Query 'what instruments are part of OSIRIS-REx' returns instrument entities via single JOIN on entity_relationships
harvest_runs has row with source='pds4' and accurate counts
entity_dictionary backward compatibility maintained
pytest tests/test_harvest_pds4.py passes with 0 failures

name: New SPDF/CDAWeb harvester
description: Create scripts/harvest_spdf.py that: (1) uses ResilientClient to fetch dataset list from CDAWeb REST API at https://cdaweb.gsfc.nasa.gov/WS/cdasr/1/dataviews/sp_phys/datasets, (2) harvests observatories from /observatories and instruments from /instruments endpoints, (3) stores datasets in datasets table with source='spdf' and CDAWeb dataset ID as canonical_id, (4) stores observatories and instruments in entities table with discipline='heliophysics', (5) creates entity_relationships: dataset->from_instrument, instrument->at_observatory, (6) cross-references SPASE ResourceIDs in entity_identifiers with id_scheme='spase_resource_id', (7) logs harvest_run. Write comprehensive tests with mocked API responses.

Uses ResilientClient for all HTTP requests
After harvest, datasets table has rows with source='spdf' and CDAWeb IDs
Entities table has observatories and instruments with discipline='heliophysics'
entity_relationships has observatory-instrument-dataset hierarchy
SPASE ResourceIDs stored in entity_identifiers with id_scheme='spase_resource_id' where available
harvest_runs has row with source='spdf' and accurate counts
pytest tests/test_harvest_spdf.py passes with 0 failures

name: Entity resolution module
description: Create src/scix/entity_resolver.py with an EntityResolver class that resolves text mentions to canonical entities. Resolution order: (1) exact match on entities.canonical_name (case-insensitive via lower()), (2) alias lookup via entity_aliases (case-insensitive via lower(alias)), (3) entity_identifiers lookup (exact match on external_id), (4) discipline-aware ranking when multiple matches (caller provides optional discipline parameter), (5) optional fuzzy fallback via pg_trgm similarity (threshold configurable, default 0.3). Returns list of EntityCandidate(entity_id, canonical_name, entity_type, source, discipline, confidence, match_method) sorted by confidence descending. Never LIMIT 1 — always return all candidates. The module should work with a psycopg connection parameter.
acceptance: File src/scix/entity_resolver.py exists with EntityResolver class
resolve('Bennu') returns candidate with canonical_name='Bennu' or '(101955) Bennu' via exact or alias match
resolve('1999 RQ36') returns Bennu via alias lookup with confidence < 1.0 (alias match)
resolve('Mars') returns multiple candidates from different sources, ranked by discipline when discipline parameter provided
resolve('MAHLI') returns Mars Hand Lens Imager via alias match
All candidates have entity_id, canonical_name, entity_type, source, discipline, confidence, match_method fields
Results sorted by confidence descending; never returns LIMIT 1
pytest tests/test_entity_resolver.py passes with 0 failures

name: Document-entity linking pipeline
description: Create src/scix/link_entities.py with a batch linking pipeline that: (1) reads extracted mentions from the extractions table (payload JSONB), (2) for each paper's mentions, calls EntityResolver to resolve against entities, (3) writes results to document_entities with match_method, confidence, and evidence (containing text mention and extraction_type), (4) processes in chunks (configurable, default 1000 papers per commit) for chunked commits, (5) is resumable: tracks last processed bibcode and skips already-linked papers on restart, (6) handles duplicate prevention via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on PK. Create CLI entrypoint that accepts --batch-size and --resume flags. Write tests with synthetic extractions data and mock resolver.
acceptance: File src/scix/link_entities.py exists with link_entities_batch() function or similar
Pipeline reads from extractions table and writes to document_entities table
Each document_entities row has: bibcode, entity_id, link_type, confidence, match_method, evidence JSONB
Chunked commits: processes N papers then commits, not one giant transaction
Resumable: after interruption, re-running skips already-processed bibcodes without duplicates
ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING prevents duplicate rows on re-run
CLI accepts --batch-size and --resume flags
pytest tests/test_link_entities.py passes with 0 failures
